AI Weekly Digest

Your curated roundup of the week's most important AI developments.

April 6, 2026 · Issue #47

This Week's Links

Scaling Laws Revisited: New Findings on LLM Efficiency — Researchers publish updated scaling estimates suggesting compute efficiency gains are outpacing earlier predictions.

Agentic Systems in Production: Lessons from Six Months of Deployment — A retrospective on running multi-agent pipelines in production, covering failure modes and cost control strategies.

Open-Source Alternatives to Proprietary Embedding Models — Benchmark results show open models are now competitive with proprietary offerings on retrieval tasks at significantly lower inference cost.

When RAG Hurts More Than It Helps — An analysis of retrieval-augmented generation failure modes, with actionable guidance on when to skip RAG entirely.

The State of Prompt Engineering in 2026 — A survey of current best practices: structured output, chain-of-thought, and the emerging role of system prompt versioning in production teams.
